content: Finally got around to rebuild the devshell in the nix repository, as I needed something with a working clang to test my code on arm. @Benno Lossin @Andreas Hindborg could you look over the pr? https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/nix/pull/8
I was not even aware of this repo - but very cool!

It's old, very old. was from before nixpkgs was able to build a rust kernel (and before rust was in mainline). always planed to update it, also want to read the ability to e.g. build the rust documentation, but so far only got to add this again as I need it to build a arm kernel without being on arm :)

But that is both with my and your new dev-shell, so maybe it's unrelated?
sounds unrelated. did you try clean?
yeah I tried it after a make clean && make mrproper, but now I created another worktree and there it does work... :thinking:
I suspect that the interpreter of fixdep was removed since you last build that. if you still have the files you can check with patchelf or something
do you know which files I need to remove in order for the build to succeed?
the shell seems to work great, so thanks a lot for that!
try git clean -xfd -- tools
ah one thing for the shell, I am getting sh: line 1: llvm-objdump: command not found, so maybe that is still needed in the shell
(I can also put that in the PR, if you want it all in one place)
have a bit on my worktree left currently (got it to also be building on macos)

Ah I see... Thanks!

I'm having issues when building arm64 defconfig with nix on x86_64. I get:

# CC [M]  arch/arm64/lib/xor-neon.o
In file included from /home/aeh/src/linux-rust/configfs/arch/arm64/lib/xor-neon.c:11:
/home/aeh/src/linux-rust/configfs/arch/arm64/include/asm/neon-intrinsics.h:33:10: fatal error: 'arm_neon.h' file not found

Did anyone come across this? I think it is a problem with my llvm not having the appropriate system include path.

@Miguel Ojeda @Gary Guo I figured out what was wrong at my end. It was two issues on op of each other.

1) If you build arch/arm64/lib/xor-neon.o directly with make ... arch/arm64/lib/xor-neon.o, flags are not set correctly. But building the file as part of a full kernel build works fine. This was my test case and thus I was blind as to what worked. Tooling from kernel.org work fine in my setup when building the full kernel.

2) The default clang in nix is built with gcc and uses gcc resources at runtime. This includes some include headers and I believe also parts of libexec. So I was getting arm_neon.h from gcc, which did not work. Using instead pkgs.llvmPackages.clangUseLLVM, the cross build works fine.
Andreas Hindborg has marked this topic as resolved.
